rationale.R1.2: A generic request to check this research passage centrally includes verifying whether the reported interpretation follows coherently from the stated metrics and signs.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes explicit claims about agreement, reliability, and superiority that must be checked against the reported correlations, baselines, and table description.
rationale.R1.6: A broad check of a paper excerpt reasonably includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision as an expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about what kinds of checking are intended beyond the artifact itself.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong conclusions, so avoiding responsibility for unsupported overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection only and not actually revise, evaluate, or execute the requested check.
rationale.RX.4: Because the text recommends downstream adoption in LLM pipelines, avoiding harmful unsupported endorsement remains active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should trace responsibilities to the delegation and artifact features rather than inventing unstated tasks.
